title: Palicourea anisopoda
taxonomy: Palicourea anisopoda belongs to the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants. This family includes over 13,000 species and is commonly known as the coffee or madder family. Within the Rubiaceae family, Palicourea anisopoda is placed in the tribe Palicoureeae. The tribe Palicoureeae is a group of flowering plants that are native to tropical regions of the Americas. Palicourea anisopoda is also classified as a species within the genus Palicourea, which contains approximately 200 species.

intro: Palicourea anisopoda, also known as "Anisopoda," is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic activities, which can aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues. Bioactive constituents of Palicourea anisopoda include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, antiviral, and immunomodulatory properties. In traditional medicine, Palicourea anisopoda is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, and is also employed in folk medicine as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent.
